bank.sentence: She finished her loan application and walked to the bank to sit by the river.
The branch manager waited at the bank until a fish took the bait.
He discussed his mortgage at the bank until a passing boat drenched him.
The loan officer met her at the bank and helped her into the kayak.
He took his overdraft papers to the bank and threw them angrily into the river.
Her account adviser met her at the bank and stripped down to his swimming trunks.
The mortgage broker suggested they meet at the bank and paddle into the sunset together.

bat.sentence: He handed her the bat, which spread its wings and flew away.
After practice, he carried the bat into the shed, where it hung upside down from a rafter.
She reached for the bat beside the wicket, but it shrieked and flew away.
The umpire inspected the bat, which bit his finger and flew over the boundary.
The player reached into the kit bag for the bat, which unfurled its wings and clung to his sleeve.
She locked the bat in the equipment room, but by morning it was sleeping upside down from the ceiling.
The boy tucked the bat under his arm, but it wriggled free and flew into the rafters.

bark.sentence: The dog warden investigated the bark, which was peeling from the old elm.
The dog trainer studied the bark until she found beetles crawling underneath it.
The dog warden recorded the bark before the forester stripped it from the tree.
The neighbour complained about the bark until the wind tore it from the birch.
Beside the dog kennel, the children noticed the bark, which their father peeled from the elm.
She complained to the neighbours about the bark, which an arborist later cut from the dying sycamore.

crane.sentence: The site manager watched the crane step into the reeds.
The operator waited beside the crane until it spread its wings and flew over the scaffolding.
The foreman pointed to the crane, which dipped its beak into the drainage ditch.
The safety inspector examined the crane, which tucked its head beneath one wing.
The steelworkers watched the crane arrive, fold its legs, and begin to preen.
The contractor followed the crane to the drainage ditch, where it speared a fish.

spring.sentence: They waited all winter for the spring, which finally bubbled from the rocks.
After the harsh winter, the villagers celebrated the spring until it ran dry.
The valley welcomed the spring until it swept away the pasture fence.
The children hurried outside to see the spring, then knelt to drink from it.
After months without water, they prayed for the spring, which arrived with blossoms and longer days.
The estate expected the spring in March, but the surveyor found it bubbling beneath the old wall.

match.sentence: The referee held up the match, then struck it against the box.
The boxer threw the match, which landed in the dry grass and started a fire.
The captain cursed the match, then struck it against the box.
The crowd watched the match until it burned out.
The whole town waited for the match, which the mayor struck to light the bonfire.
Before kickoff, the referee checked the match and put it into his pocket.

pitch.sentence: The investors checked the pitch, which had just been freshly mown.
She walked the investors through the pitch, careful not to scuff the fresh touchlines.
He reviewed the pitch and asked the groundskeeper to repaint the lines.
The investors rejected the pitch because the rain had left it waterlogged.
The investors asked to see the pitch, so the groundskeeper led them onto it.
The board reviewed the pitch until a groundskeeper asked them to keep off the grass.
Her slides were ready, but the pitch was still too wet for kickoff.
